_comment: Identity Attack & Recovery Simulator — scenario config v0.1
_usage: Simulator reads this file at load time. Update image paths and text here without touching simulator code.

scenario.attacker_app_tagline: Because overspending on licenses doesn't make cents.
scenario.attacker_app_value_prop: Our AI analyzes your tenant usage patterns in real-time to save you up to 30% on your license costs.

lure_page._comment: Screen 1 — fake landing page the victim sees after clicking the LinkedIn ad

logo._placeholder: PLACEHOLDER — drop cleaned logo crop here

hero_image._placeholder: Screen 1 — Nano-Cents AI landing page with Connect with Microsoft 365 CTA

lure_page.footer_text: By connecting, you agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
testimonials: Saved us 28% on licensing in 90 days — IT Director, Financial Services
Identified 400 unused seats we didn't know we had — CTO, Healthcare
Paid for itself in the first month — VP Infrastructure, Retail

device_code_page._comment: Screen 2 — device code phishing page shown after victim clicks Connect

logo._placeholder: PLACEHOLDER — same logo as lure page

device_code_page.instruction_text: To verify your identity and for use to securely connect, please visit:
device_code_page.spinner_label: Waiting for you to sign in...

screenshot._placeholder: Screen 2 — device code page with IXA9RZ3NL code and waiting spinner

microsoft_consent_page._comment: Screen 3 — real Microsoft device login page (shown as a mockup/screenshot embed)

screenshot._placeholder: Screen 3 — real Microsoft Enter code to allow access page

microsoft_consent_page.warning_text: Do not enter codes from sources you don't trust.
microsoft_consent_page.narrator_callout: This is a real Microsoft page. The warning is real. The victim ignores it because the previous page looked legitimate.

placeholders._comment: When an image src is empty, simulator renders these SVG placeholders instead. Replace src values above when screenshots are ready.

attack_stages._comment: Labels and timing used by the attack simulation tab. Edit text here, not in the HTML.

stage1.label: Consent Granted
stage1.short: OAuth token issued via device code flow

stage2.label: Persistence Established
stage2.short: Service principal registered with admin consent

stage3.label: Identity Control Plane Damaged
stage3.short: Backdoor admin created · CA disabled · MFA stripped

stage4.label: Lateral SaaS Movement
stage4.short: M365 files corrupted · Salesforce records deleted

recovery_steps._comment: Labels for the five recovery steps. Edit here to match your narrative.
